rawInput: Second call with Slate Data and Sofia keeps circling back to whether we can actually hold their peak throughput — they run big nightly batch jobs and she's worried about hitting API rate limits at scale in production. It's not a no, but she's clearly been burned by an API provider throttling them before. Same capacity-at-scale anxiety I keep seeing — not sure if it's a real blocker yet or just scar tissue I should note and move past.

Anika at Cedarline asked three separate times about sustained tokens-per-minute and whether they'd hit API rate limits at scale when their traffic spikes. They're at 2,800 people and scaling fast. Capacity-at-scale anxiety is the thing sitting under all the technical questions, more than model quality. Flagging it because I keep hearing this shape and don't have a clean place to file it.

Paul from Brightwall wants a concrete throughput SLA before he'll recommend us internally — kept saying 'we can't have the thing that powers our product hitting API rate limits at scale during a launch.' Same capacity-at-scale anxiety I'm hearing on Cedarline. Not a hard objection, but it's clearly load-bearing for him.

Raj at Northpeak keeps asking how they'd customize Claude for their domain — fine-tuning, adapters, that kind of thing. Honestly I don't know how to position our fine-tuning and customization story against what OpenAI is pitching them. I need a clearer answer from product before the next call.

Kevin at Slate asked whether they can fine-tune on their proprietary schema data. I gave a vague answer about prompt engineering and long context, but I could tell it didn't land. I don't know how to position our fine-tuning and customization story to technical buyers — I keep fumbling this one and it's starting to cost me credibility.
